summary: Phenomenal early‑'80 Chicago show with a soaring China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider, captured on a bright balcony‑rail audience tape of excellent clarity.
review: The recording is praised as crystal‑clear and full of energy despite being an audience (balcony‑rail) source, and the second set – highlighted by a spectacular China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider and a fiery Rooster performance – is described as one of the reviewer’s personal favorites. All three reviewers give five stars, noting strong playing from Jerry, Phil and Bob, and lamenting only the missing tail‑end of the second set.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviews are uniformly enthusiastic (5 stars), lauding both the musical performance and the surprisingly high‑quality audience recording, with no substantive criticism.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Balcony‑rail (audience) mic capture; bright, clear sound with excellent instrument separation. No major dropouts noted, though the tail end of the second set is missing.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set is the highlight – high energy, seamless transitions (notably Jerry’s guitar burst) and a memorable China Cat > Rider sequence. The show maintains momentum throughout despite the missing closing portion of the second set.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar work is described as flawless, with a “burst open” solo that lifts the jam.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass is loud and proud, driving the high‑energy feel of the night.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob (Bobby) delivers a Chess‑style performance on "The Rooster," adding vocal grit and excitement.
